title: Orbán’s long rule ends as Hungary votes for change — and for an uncertain future
standfirst: Viktor Orbán’s 16-year grip on power has been broken by Péter Magyar, a former insider who turned public fatigue into a political tidal wave. The result is more than a change of leadership: it is a verdict on a style of government that reshaped Hungary’s institutions, strained its ties with the European Union and left many voters longing for relief rather than revolution.
